transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good evening, First National Bank. My name is Zara. Could I take your name and date of birth for security?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yeah — Hana Yamamoto, thirty-first of January 1995.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Hana, you're verified. How can I help?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: So I tried to make an online purchase earlier — about two hundred and forty pounds — and my card kept coming up as declined. I've got plenty of money so I'm confused.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Let me check the transaction history. I can see two declined attempts for that amount. The merchant is an international site — the transactions were flagged by our international fraud prevention controls. We see a higher rate of fraud on cross-border e-commerce.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: So my card isn't blocked — it's specifically flagged for that site?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Not permanently. It was an automatic hold on that transaction pattern. I can whitelist international e-commerce for your account for the next thirty days, which should allow the payment to go through. Would you like me to do that?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Done. I'd also recommend enabling transaction notifications in the app — that way you'll see in real time if anything unusual happens. Your card will now work for that purchase.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: It's gone through. Thank you so much. You're a star.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Brilliant! Is there anything else I can help with?
